front: Movement: second movement (Adagio assai) of this symphony is a funeral march; later quoted by Richard Strauss in Metamorphosen
Movement: third movement (Lento) of this piano sonata in B-flat minor is a funeral march featuring a repeating F–G-flat motif below a B-flat pedal, giving the sonata its nickname
Movement: third movement of this symphony quotes 'Frère Jacques' as a funeral march
Movement: Gounod wrote a funeral march 'for a marionette'; funeral music also appears in Act 3 of this Wagner opera after Siegfried's death
back: Funeral March of a Marionette (Gounod) / Götterdämmerung (Wagner)

front: Suite: six military marches by Elgar; the first march's trio contains 'Land of Hope and Glory,' commonly heard at graduation ceremonies worldwide
Suite: first march premiered at Yale University (arranged by Samuel Sanford for Elgar's honorary doctorate), cementing it as a graduation staple
March genre: very fast circus marches called 'screamers'; the best-known screamer is Julius Fučík's Entry of the Gladiators; formal structure features strains bridged to trios by 'dogfights'
work: March as Genre – Structure and Subtypes

front: March genre: William Walton wrote Crown Imperial and Orb and Sceptre for British coronations; Tchaikovsky wrote the Marche Slave and a 'Jurist's March' in D
work: March as Genre – Structure and Subtypes
